questions.text: The sex of a child is determined by:
questions.explanation: The father determines the sex of the child. If sperm carries X chromosome, child is female (XX). If Y chromosome, child is male (XY).
Acquired traits (like skills, scars) are not inherited. Only genetic traits in DNA are passed to offspring.

questions.correctAnswer: Organs with different origin but similar function.
questions.explanation: Analogous organs have different basic structure but perform similar functions. Example: wings of bird and insect.

questions.text: The theory of natural selection was proposed by ________.
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin proposed the theory of natural selection in his book 'On the Origin of Species' (1859).
